name: Heavenly Heart Cake
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: www.hersheys.com
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour two heart-shaped pans or two 9-inch round baking pans.
2. In a small bowl, stir together cocoa and boiling water until smooth. Set aside.
3. In a large bowl, beat butter and sugar with an electric mixer until fluffy. Beat in eggs and cocoa mixture.
4. Stir together flour, baking soda and salt; add alternately with buttermilk to the butter mixture.
5. Pour batter into prepared pans. Bake 30-35 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool pans for 10 minutes before removing cakes from wire racks. Allow cakes to cool completely.
6. Prepare GLOSSY CHOCOLATE SOUR CREAM FROSTING and CREAMY BUTTERCREAM FROSTING as directed on their respective pages.
7. Frost the cake layers with GLOSSY CHOCOLATE SOUR CREAM FROSTING. Pipe a border around the top edge and base of the cake with CREAMY BUTTERCREAM FROSTING. Garnish as desired. Serve 10 to 12 portions.
To make sour milk: Combine 2 teaspoons white vinegar with milk until equal to 3/4 cup.
GLOSSY CHOCOLATE SOUR CREAM FROSTING:
In a medium microwave-safe bowl, place chocolate chips. Microwave at HIGH (100%) for 1 minute; stir. If necessary, continue microwaving in 15-second intervals, stirring after each heating, until chips are melted when stirred. Add powdered sugar, sour cream and vanilla; beat until smooth. Add milk, 1/2 teaspoon at a time, to achieve desired consistency.
CREAMY BUTTERCREAM FROSTING:
In a small bowl, combine powdered sugar, softened butter or margarine (1/4 cup), milk (2 tablespoons) and vanilla extract. Beat until smooth and creamy.
